finding_results.notes: Peter retains a controlled formal baseline, but peak-pressure turns now fracture syntax (self-corrections and doubled clauses) and include a stressed contraction ("I can't") to break monotone legalistic cadence.

Call-block idiolects are separated: Elsie now uses compressed domestic-task shorthand (kettle, towels, lane, immediate actions), while Keane is framed through threshold-based clinical triage language plus explicit consent check ("Do I have your yes?").

summary: All packet findings for the dialogue/idiolect/cadence pass were addressed in-place in chapter 5 with minimal collateral churn while preserving continuity, heading contract, and chapter consequences.
